Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex A sophisticated yet basic-skilled threat actor group C0021 targets the communications sector with a multi-stage attack plan involving extensive reconnaissance, development of resources, and multiple phases of intrusion to achieve their objectives.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Command Interpreter (Bash), Command & Control Server, Public IP/Network Block, Domain Name Registrar, Logon Session, DNS Record, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Bash Script, Web Shell, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Remote Desktop Protocol, Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the target's network infrastructure by analyzing file metadata to identify potential weak points and resources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers establish servers or acquire third-party services to host phishing emails and malware, preparing the groundwork for the attack.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Specific individuals within the organization receive tailored phishing emails containing malicious links, leading to initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once inside, attackers use command-line interfaces to execute malicious payloads crafted as bash scripts.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain persistence, attackers install a web shell on a compromised server to allow remote control.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges and gain administrative control.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Defenses are impaired by disabling antivirus software and other protective measures to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Attackers attempt to gain valid credentials through repeated login attempts using password guessing or brute force.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Mapping the internal network helps identify valuable targets and resources for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using RDP, attackers move laterally across the network to access other systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Attackers extract sensitive data from internal repositories like SharePoint or file shares.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is transferred out of the network using less common protocols to evade detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Attackers destroy or manipulate critical data to simulate the impact of real-world attacks and disrupt operations.

Scenario.summary: The complex attack orchestrated by the C0021 group involves meticulous planning and execution, targeting the communications sector with a multi-faceted approach. From initial reconnaissance to data exfiltration, the attackers employ a variety of techniques to infiltrate, persist, and ultimately impact the targeted organization.
